perturbation.original_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)).
perturbation.changed_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall only refer to information explicitly marked as “Confidential” in writing at the time of disclosure by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing. Any other information is not considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines Confidential Information broadly. The modified text significantly narrows the definition, requiring explicit written designation at the time of disclosure. This creates a contradiction because information that would have previously been considered confidential under the original definition is now excluded, creating uncertainty about which information is protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall keep strictly confidential and shall not disclose, or cause or permit to be disclosed, to any person or entity, (i) any information about a potential transaction between Recipient and the Company (the “ Transaction ”) or the fact that Recipient has received, or may receive, Confidential Information and is considering the Transaction and all discussions between the Company and Recipient related thereto, including the existence of this Agreement, except that (subject to Section 2.2 below) Recipient may make such disclosure if it has received the reasonable written advice of its outside counsel that such disclosure must be made in order that Recipient not commit a violation of law, and (ii) the Confidential Information, except to those officers, employees or other authorized Representatives (as defined herein) and who shall agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement, and except as otherwise consented to in writing by the Company. Recipient shall take all actions reasonably necessary to ensure that the Confidential Information remains strictly confidential and is not disclosed to or seen, used or obtained by any person or entity except in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Recipient agrees not to contact any shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company or its affiliates with respect to the Transaction or for the purpose of obtaining information for use in evaluating the Transaction, without the Company’s prior written consent. Recipient further agrees that all inquiries, requests for information and other communications concerning the Transaction shall be made only through Guggenheim Securities, LLC (“Guggenheim”). Company agrees that, without Recipient’s prior written consent, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that Recipient is considering the Transaction, that this Agreement exists, that the Confidential Information has been made available to Recipient, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto including the status thereof, the valuation, or indicative offers, or proposals.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The changed text drastically narrows the definition, requiring explicit written designation at the time of disclosure. The contradicted text obligates the recipient to keep 'Confidential Information' strictly confidential. Since the changed text significantly limits what qualifies as Confidential Information, it directly contradicts the contradicted text's expectation of confidentiality for a broader range of information. The change means the recipient is no longer obligated to keep information confidential that it previously would have been, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, immediately destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and retain none for its files. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will be required to erase electronically stored Confidential Information that has been saved to a back-up file or other electronic medium in accordance with its or its Representatives’ ordinary back-up practices. Notwithstanding such return or destruction, Recipient shall continue to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, decide at their sole discretion to destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and decide whether to retain for its files. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will be required to erase electronically stored Confidential Information that has been saved to a back-up file or other electronic medium in accordance with its or its Representatives’ ordinary back-up practices. Recipient shall not continue to be bound by this Agreement after returning the Confidential information
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of Confidential Information. The changed text gives the Recipient discretion to decide whether to return or destroy the information, directly contradicting the original requirement. Furthermore, it is changing the effect of returning the Confidential information. In the original text it states that the Recipient will continue to be bound by the NDA, but with the added perturbation this condition is being revoked and that the agreement ceases to have effect
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that its failure to perform any obligation or duty which it has agreed to perform under this Agreement will cause irreparable harm to the Company, which harm cannot be adequately compensated for by money damages. It is further agreed by Recipient that an order of specific performance or for injunctive relief against Recipient in the event of a breach or default under the terms of this Agreement would be equitable and would not work a hardship on Recipient. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or default by Recipient hereunder, the Company, without any bond or other security being required and in addition to whatever other remedies are or might be available at law or in equity, shall have the right either to compel specific performance by, or to obtain injunctive relief against, Recipient, with respect to any obligation or duty herein or breach thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates the destruction or return of confidential information, while the changed text gives the Recipient sole discretion to decide whether to return or destroy the information. This contradicts the original requirement. Furthermore, the changed text states that the agreement ceases to have effect after returning the Confidential Information, whereas the original text clearly states that the Recipient shall continue to be bound by the Agreement. This contradicts the contradicted text, which provides remedies such as specific performance and injunctive relief in the event of a breach or default, because the changed text makes the contradicted text unenforceable after returning the information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives from and against any and all losses, damages, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) caused by or arising out of any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any breach for which Recipient is responsible hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives from and against any and all losses, damages, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys’ fees and expenses) caused by or arising out of any breach of this Agreement by Recipient or any breach for which Recipient is responsible hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the recipient 'shall' indemnify the company. Replacing 'shall' with 'may' introduces discretion where there was none before. Now the recipient has an option, which directly contradicts the original intent of the clause which was to impose a firm requirement
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon, and inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by, the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns, but this Agreement shall not be assignable by Recipient without the prior written consent of the Company. This Agreement constitutes the complete agreement between the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and shall continue in full force and effect until terminated by mutual agreement of the parties hereto. The section headings used herein are for reference purposes only and shall not in any way affect the meaning or interpretation of this Agreement. This Agreement shall be construed, performed and enforced in accordance with, and governed by, the internal laws of the State of Maryland, without giving effect to the principles of conflicts of law thereof, and each party consents to personal jurisdiction in such state and voluntarily submits to the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in Baltimore, Maryland, in any action or proceeding relating to this Agreement. Whenever possible, each provision of this Agreement shall be interpreted in such a manner as to be effective and valid under applicable law, but if any provision hereof is held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable under any applicable law or rule in any jurisdiction, such provision will be ineffective only to the extent of such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability, without invalidating the remainder of this Agreement. This Agreement may not be modified or amended and no provision hereof may be waived, in whole or in part, except by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto. No waiver of any breach or default hereunder shall be considered valid unless in writing, and no such waiver shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or similar nature. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Recipient shall indemnify', which is a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'Recipient may indemnify', introducing an optional element. This directly contradicts the 'enforceable' aspect of the agreement as stated in the contradicted text: 'This Agreement shall be binding upon, and inure to the benefit of, and be enforceable by, the parties hereto'. Because the changed text now gives the Recipient discretion regarding indemnification, it undermines the enforceability of the agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The KeyW Holding Corporation Jacobs Engineering Group Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: The KeyW Holding Corporation Acme Corp Inc.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the counter-party name will create a lot of contradiction, as it is changing the agreement to an agreement that it is not between KeyW and Jacobs but between KeyW and Acme. This will create a conflict with references to "Recipient" throughout the document, as those references are intended to refer to Jacobs Engineering Group Inc., not Acme Corp Inc
perturbation.contradicted_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)). Confidential Information also includes any notes, analyses, compilations, data, forecasts, reports, summaries, studies or other material or documents prepared by Recipient which contain, reflect or are based, in whole or in part, on the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Confidential Information shall not include information or material that (i) is publicly available or becomes publicly available through no action or fault of Recipient, (ii) was already in Recipient’s possession or known to Recipient prior to being disclosed or provided to Recipient by or on behalf of the Company, provided , that, the source of such information or material was not bound by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the Company or any other party with respect thereto, (iii) was or is obtained by Recipient from a third party, provided , that, such third party was not bound by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the Company or any other party with respect to such information or material, or (iv) is independently developed by the Recipient without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the agreement is between KeyW and Jacobs. The changed text alters this, stating the agreement is now between KeyW and Acme. The contradicted text refers to "Recipient" as receiving confidential information from the company. By changing the counterparty from Jacobs to Acme, the 'Recipient' in the contradicted text now ambiguously refers to Acme, when it was originally intended to refer to Jacobs. This creates a direct contradiction, as the confidential information clause would be inappropriately applied to a different entity than originally intended.

perturbation.original_text: Unless approved in advance in writing by the board of directors of the Company, the Recipient agrees that it will not, for a period of one (1) year after the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly:
perturbation.changed_text: Unless approved in advance orally by the board of directors of the Company, the Recipient agrees that it will not, for a period of twenty (20) year after the date of this Agreement, directly or indirectly:
perturbation.explanation: Changing the agreement conditions from "in writing" to "orally", and time for enforcement from "one (1) year" to "twenty (20) year" will create a contradiction because it imposes a longer standstill period based on non documented or unprovable agreement, that it is supposed to be for one year, and now it is 20 years
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the enforcement period from one (1) year to twenty (20) years, and the approval from 'in writing' to 'orally.' The contradicted text states that the agreement is in full force and effect for two (2) years. Since the changed text now requires the Recipient to comply with a twenty (20) year standstill period after the agreement, it directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement is only valid for two (2) years, creating a structural flaw and in-text contradiction. Furthermore, changing 'in writing' to 'orally' imposes agreement conditions based on non documented or unprovable terms, that should supposed to be for one year based on written record

perturbation.original_text: The Company grants no licenses, by implication or otherwise, under any patent, copyright, trademark, intellectual property rights, trade secret or other rights by disclosing Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company grants full licenses, by implication or otherwise, under any patent, copyright, trademark, intellectual property rights, trade secret or other rights by disclosing Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no licenses are granted by disclosing confidential information. The modified text claims that *full* licenses *are* granted by disclosing confidential information. This is a direct contradiction. It also contradicts clause 2.3, about the Ownership of the Company's confidendial informaiton
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Confidential Information is owned solely and exclusively by the Company and shall remain the exclusive property of the Company. No right, title or interest in or to any of the Confidential Information or any material developed therefrom is transferred to Recipient hereby or by its delivery to Recipient hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no licenses are granted by disclosing confidential information, whereas the changed text says that full licenses *are* granted. The contradicted text states that the Confidential Information is owned solely and exclusively by the Company and no right, title or interest is transferred to Recipient. The change directly contradicts this by granting licenses to the Recipient upon disclosure. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Company cannot both exclusively own the information *and* grant full licenses to it.
